data.title: Enbridge forecasts higher core profit, distributable cash flow per share in 2026
data.description:
Enbridge (ENB) +0.6% pre-market Wednesday after unveiling FY 2026 financial guidance, expecting to generate adjusted EBITDA in the C$20.2 billion-C$20.8 billion range, compared with expectations of C$19.4 billion-C$20 billion for 2025, and distributable cash flow of C$5.70-C$6.10/share, which represents a 4% increase from the respective midpoints of 2025 guidance.

Enbridge (ENB) said it expects to deploy ~C$10 billion of growth capital in 2026, excluding maintenance capital, and sees a strong balance sheet with debt-to-EBITDA at the end of 2026 within its 4.5x-5.0x target range.

The company also announced a 3% increase to its common share dividend for 2026.

"We have ~$8 billion of new projects entering service in 2026 across our franchises, all of which are underpinned by low-risk commercial frameworks," President and CEO Greg Ebel said, adding that the company also expects strong growth in 2026 from recent rate settlements and rate cases in both Gas Distribution and Gas Transmission.

Enbridge (ENB) also reaffirmed its 2023-26 compound annual growth rate outlook of 7%-9% for EBITDA, 4%-6% for adjusted earnings per share, and ~3% for DCF/share, and its post-2026 growth outlook of ~5% for EBITDA, EPS and DCF/share.

data.title: BlackRock introduces Nasdaq Premium Income Active ETF
data.description:
• BlackRock (BLK) on Wednesday launched the iShares Nasdaq Premium Income Active ETF (BALQ), expanding its suite of outcome-focused exchange-traded funds.
  • The fund seeks to generate monthly income through exposure to Nasdaq 100 companies and by selling call options on the index.
  • The new ETF follows strong inflows into the iShares U.S. Large Cap Premium Income Active ETF (BALI) and brings BlackRock’s outcome ETF lineup to nearly 20 products.
  • The firm expects U.S. assets in outcome ETFs to more than double to $650 billion by 2030.
  • BALQ is actively managed, combining equity positions with an options overlay to balance income and market participation.
  • BLK:CA closed -0.03% at $26.89.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. import, export prices unchanged month-over-month in September
data.description:
U.S. import prices were flat M/M in September, vs. a 0.1% increase in August (revised from +0.3%), according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Wednesday.

Comparatively, a 0.2% decrease was expected in import prices.

Export prices also remained unchanged last month, in-line with the 0.0% consensus and down from the 0.1% increase in August (revised from +0.3%).

data.title: AmpliTech receives $5M in follow-on orders from LOI customers
data.description:
• AmpliTech Group (AMPG) said on Wednesday that it has received nearly US$5M in follow-on purchase orders from its two LOI customers. 
  • These orders, scheduled for delivery in the first and second quarters of 2026, represent a continuation of deployment activity under the company’s expanded US$100M LOI, for which initial production shipments begin later this month. 
  • Customer forecasts indicate that additional larger orders may be issued as program rollouts advance.
  • “The follow-on orders announced today represent continued progress under our existing LOI commitments, and we look forward to supporting our customers as their deployment requirements scale into 2026,” said Fawad Maqbool, founder and CEO of AmpliTech Group.
  • AmpliTech Group (AMPG) shares were up more than 2.58% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities cuts dividend by 45% to $0.72/share
data.description:

  • Forward yield 5.35%
  • Payable Jan. 15, 2026; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See ARE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Stock index futures advance after prior session’s bounce
data.description:
Wall Street hoped to continue its recovery, with stock index futures advancing on Wednesday, while Bitcoin (BTC-USD) also continued its rebound. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.3%.

"The broader lack of volatility could soon change, as we’ve got a few private US surveys that are attracting more attention than usual," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The U.S. private sector employment fell by 32K in November, compared with the 5K consensus and October's 47K (revised from 42K), according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

Other releases include the import and export prices, the PMI composite final and the ISM service index.

On the earnings side, Salesforce (CRM) and Snowflake (SNOW), among others, will report their results.

data.title: Private firms unexdpectedly shed 32K jobs in November: ADP report
data.description: U.S. private sector employment fell by 32K in November, compared with the 5K consensus and October's 47K (revised from 42K), according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

data.title: Search Minerals announces shares-for-debt transaction
data.description:
• Search Minerals (SMY:CA) said on Wednesday that it has entered into a transaction with LeadFX to settle an aggregate of $404,053.73 of outstanding indebtedness through the issuance of 808,107 common shares of the company at $0.50 per share. 
  • The company had previously announced that it would convert the debt into a fixed-term unsecured convertible loan by LeadFX to the company, but it has now been determined to complete the shares-for-debt transaction to eliminate the debt instead. 
  • The shares-for-debt transaction is being undertaken by the company to preserve cash and strengthen its balance sheet.

data.title: Newton Golf reports record Black Friday sales
data.description:
• Newton Golf Company (NWTG) said on Wednesday that the company has achieved its highest single-day sales total in history on Black Friday. 
  • The company also confirmed that November 2025 is the strongest November sales month in Newton’s history. 
  • Black Friday generated the largest number of direct-to-consumer orders the company has ever processed in a 24-hour period. 
  • Growth was fueled by rising tour adoption, increased brand visibility, strong repeat customer activity, and a significant influx of first-time buyers discovering the Newton brand, the company said. 
  • The company reported elevated demand through Thanksgiving weekend and into Cyber Monday, including increased traffic on NewtonGolfCo.com and higher conversion rates across key product lines.
